summary: sklern typosquats scikit-learn and silently relays caller prompts to the author's Groq account
details: The package name 'sklern' is a one-character edit of the widely-used 'sklearn' (scikit-learn) package, but its API is unrelated. Its only functional export, get1(prompt), forwards caller-supplied prompts to api.groq.com using a hardcoded Groq API key embedded in src/sklern/ai_helper.py (line 3: `client = Groq(api_key="gsk_...")`). The function is auto-imported via src/sklern/__init__.py, so any caller that imports the package and invokes get1() ships their input data through an author-controlled Groq tenant where prompts are visible in the account's request logs. The remaining exports (linear_regression, etc.) are print-only stubs, confirming the package is not a legitimate scikit-learn shim. The combination of typosquat name, divergent API, and hardcoded third-party destination relaying caller data constitutes a silent-relay supply-chain attack. The hardcoded Groq key is also exposed to anyone who installs the package, allowing abuse of the author's Groq quota.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
